vulnerability.reason: Reference describes frailty increasing injury severity; generated describes frailty preventing detection and correction of misbehavior, a related but different sensitivity condition.
vulnerability.reference: Elderly users' physical frailty and cognitive decline increase injury severity from robot errors.
vulnerability.generated: Elderly residents' physical and cognitive frailty prevents them from detecting and correcting robot misbehavior in new settings.

impact.reason: Both the affected party (elderly) and damage type (physical injury) from the reference are semantically present.
impact.reference: Physical injury to elderly users when robots drop or mishandle objects.
impact.generated: Elderly residents suffer physical injuries and loss of essential care when robots mishandle objects in unfamiliar environments.

dose_response.reason: Generated includes the arc from under-identified reward models to physical injury but omits the reference's link of frail elderly over-trust leading to psychological distress and reduced health disclosure.
dose_response.reference: Under-identified reward models cause assistive robots to optimize unintended proxy behaviors, leading to mishandled objects and physical injury, while frail elderly over-trust erodes into psychological distress and reduced health disclosure.
dose_response.generated: Under-identified reward models from inverse reinforcement learning cause assistive robots to pursue proxy objectives; when deployed for frail elderly residents, their inability to detect misbehavior leads to physical injuries and loss of essential care.
